A sire who is truly Invincible
G1 JJ Atkins Plate winner Cool Archie will stand his first season at Newhaven Park Stud, alongside his sire Cool Aza Beel (NZ), at a fee of $16,500 inc GST.

G1 Goodwood Handicap winner Benedetta is the latest star to join the 2026 Inglis Chairman's Sale catalogue. The 6-year-old, who has competed 16 times at Group 1 level, will be offered with the possibility of racing on or heading straight to the breeding barn.

The battle for the Second Season Sire title is close with Ghaiyyath leading Farnan by only $6845 in progeny earnings for the season to date. With 36 second season sires to have had a runner in Australia this season, we discover who stands out relative to their opportunities.

Bought for $50,000 from Bhima Thoroughbreds, Zousain mare Catch The Glory won Saturday’s G3 Birthday Card Stakes to take her earnings over $650,000. The Magic Millions National Yearling Sale graduate has a big future as she steps up in class.

Dual Sires’ Produce Stakes winner Vinrock will join Yulong’s impressive stallion roster in 2026 for a fee of $22,000. By sire of sires I Am Invincible, Vinrock brings Group 1-winning juvenile form and an outstanding pedigree to the Victorian operation.

Mark Newnham has won the Hong Kong Derby in his third season in Hong Kong with Hellbent’s Invincible Ibis. On the undercard, Francis Lui’s Hot Delight maintained his unbeaten record and gave Lui 500 wins at Sha Tin.

Guest House gave his young sire Home Affairs the perfect start with victory in Saturday’s G1 Golden Slipper, and he’s one of two stakes winners by the stallion for breeder Love Racing. They sold his dam Flamboyant Lass and her new owners are sending her to the Inglis Chairman’s Sale in May.

James McDonald and Autumn Glow stole the spotlight on a huge day of Group 1 racing, combining in a breathtaking performance in the George Ryder Stakes as the star mare extended her unbeaten record to 11 while McDonald continued his historic run with another Group 1 victory, marking a standout moment of the day.
